risks: Single-region deployment (eastus only) with no zone redundancy — vulnerable to regional outages and does not meet WAF reliability guidance for production workloads
No VNet integration or private endpoints configured — all resources (Cosmos DB, Key Vault, Storage, ACR, ML workspace) are publicly accessible
Cosmos DB has publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled without IP or VNet restrictions, exposing the data plane to the public internet
Online endpoint uses Key-based authentication (authMode: Key) rather than AAD/managed identity, which is less secure and harder to rotate
ACR Basic tier has a 10 GiB storage limit — may constrain model image growth as the recommendation engine evolves
No diagnostic settings or resource tags defined for cost tracking, governance, or operational triage
correctionsRecommended: Add a second Cosmos DB region or enable availability zone redundancy (isZoneRedundant: true) for production reliability
Configure private endpoints for Cosmos DB, Key Vault, Storage Account, and the ML workspace to enforce network isolation
Set Cosmos DB publicNetworkAccess to Disabled once private endpoints are in place
Upgrade online endpoint authMode from Key to AADToken and use managed identity for service-to-service authentication
Add resource tags (e.g., environment, costCenter, workload) across all resources for governance and cost allocation
Consider upgrading ACR from Basic to Standard tier as model image count and size grow
Disable local (key-based) auth on the Storage Account to align with WAF security best practices
